close_reason: Added with_timeout(30s) wrapper around all SDK network calls
description: src/lib.rs:144 calls Client::new(None) and never configures a timeout. The Bitwarden SDK builds an internal reqwest client; the default reqwest client has NO timeout, only a connect timeout. A network black-hole (silently dropped packets, hung TLS handshake mid-stream, slowloris) will cause login_access_token, projects().list(), secrets().list_by_project(), or secrets().get() to hang for tokio's eternity. Other secretx backends in this workspace (e.g. azure-kv) face the same class of problem; the fix here depends on whether bitwarden::Client exposes a way to set a per-request or per-client timeout. Investigate the bitwarden 2.0.0 API: ClientSettings or similar may take a timeout. If not, surface it via a tokio::time::timeout wrapper inside fetch() with a sane default (say 30s) overridable by env var like SECRETX_BWS_TIMEOUT_SECS.
title: Bitwarden SDK calls have no per-request timeout — get() can hang indefinitely
